intro: Huberantha perrieri, also known as the "Madagascar Coral Tree," is a flowering plant in the family Annonaceae native to Madagascar. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and protect against infections. The bioactive constituents of Huberantha perrieri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
